mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds 5.0%, monetary policy is sufficiently restrictive to increase the equity risk premium and discount rate for future earnings, causing negative pressure on S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust economic activity and corporate earnings potential, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing consumer spending (negative momentum in RSAFS) and restrictive monetary policy (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) indicates that high borrowing costs are curtailing demand, leading to lower corporate revenue expectations and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The negative signal from the interaction term does not produce a lower mean 60-day forward return compared to periods where only one condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy supply side of the economy, translating directly into higher earnings for a large portion of the S&P 500 and driving positive returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently below 0.10 over multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated while the Fed Funds rate is high, it signals a policy dilemma that is negative for risk assets. The Fed cannot cut rates to support growth, leading to margin compression and lower equity prices.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are not statistically lower in periods where both trigger conditions are met compared to periods where only one or neither is met.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals a peak in the labor cycle. This condition precedes economic slowdowns as wage pressures build and corporate margins contract, causing negative forward returns as the market anticipates a downturn.
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE below 4.5% are not followed by a statistically significant decline in S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days compared to the long-term average.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in housing starts (HOUST) is a high-frequency leading indicator of broad economic confidence and activity, which positively impacts investor sentiment and drives short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of HOUST monthly changes predicting 5-day forward returns is not consistently positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.0% on a quarter-over-quarter annualized basis, it signals a significant risk of recession. This structural slowdown drives a risk-off sentiment, leading to negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In historical periods where the trigger condition was met, the S&P 500 did not experience a median 60-day return that was negative and statistically different from the unconditional median.
